name: Radar
what_it_does: Location data platform API combining geocoding, geofencing, trip tracking, and place detection, optimized for mobile applications with device-side SDKs and server-side REST APIs.
use_cases: Geofencing — detecting when users or assets enter/exit defined geographic regions
Curbside pickup tracking — monitoring customer location as they approach a retail location
Delivery ETA and trip tracking with real-time updates to both shipper and recipient
Address autocomplete for checkout flows with global address coverage
Background location monitoring for mobile apps with battery-efficient tracking
not_for: Turn-by-turn navigation or routing optimization (use TomTom or HERE)
Static mapping display without location tracking
Applications without mobile client SDKs — server-side only use is limited
best_when: You're building a mobile app that needs location intelligence features like geofencing, trip tracking, or place detection, and want a unified platform rather than piecing together multiple mapping APIs.
avoid_when: You only need geocoding without tracking, or you're building a purely server-side location pipeline without mobile clients.

auth.notes: Publishable keys for client-side SDK (can be exposed). Secret keys for server-side API (must be kept private). Test and live key pairs separate environments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 100K monthly active users; 100K geocoding calls/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $49/month; Scale with custom pricing for enterprise
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for most small-to-medium applications. Pricing based on MAUs and API calls, which can be more predictable than pure call-based pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on plan; generous for paid plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clean REST API with standard HTTP codes and descriptive JSON error bodies. Webhook payloads well-documented with event types.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Geocoding fully idempotent. Event tracking and trip updates are stateful — repeated calls advance state rather than being idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Publishable and secret key split — mixing them up causes confusing auth errors
Geofencing events require mobile SDK running on device — server-side geofence checks use different API
Trip tracking state machine is complex — understand states (started, approaching, arrived) before implementing
Radar places database is separate from Foursquare — categories and IDs don't map 1:1
Webhooks require HTTPS endpoint — local development needs tunneling
Test environment data is separate from live — easy to forget to switch environments
